headline: Korean chip stocks rebound after overnight US selloff, as chip supply remains tight
summary: Shares of South Korean chipmakers Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix rebounded on Wednesday after early losses, as investors bought the dip ‌following a sharp selloff fuelled by concerns over the sustainability of the AI-driven ‌chip boom. Samsung rose as much as 1.4% and SK Hynix climbed as much as 5.8% in morning trade, after ​earlier falling as much as 4.4% and 5%, respectively, as they initially tracked an overnight decline in U.S. semiconductor stocks. Analysts said the earnings season is only just beginning and expectations for strong results from chipmakers remain intact.

content.title: Korean chip stocks rebound after overnight US selloff, as chip supply remains tight
content.summary: Shares of South Korean chipmakers Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix rebounded on Wednesday after early losses, as investors bought the dip ‌following a sharp selloff fuelled by concerns over the sustainability of the AI-driven ‌chip boom. Samsung rose as much as 1.4% and SK Hynix climbed as much as 5.8% in morning trade, after ​earlier falling as much as 4.4% and 5%, respectively, as they initially tracked an overnight decline in U.S. semiconductor stocks. Analysts said the earnings season is only just beginning and expectations for strong results from chipmakers remain intact.

headline: SK Hynix, Samsung Drag KOSPI Into Bear Market as Memory-Chip Selloff Deepens
summary: The world’s hottest index has been dragged into a technical bear market as the memory-chip selloff gathered pace Wednesday.  The KOSPI index closed down 5.4% in South Korean trading as its two largest constituents  SK Hynix  and  Samsung Electronics  dropped 5.7% and 6.3%, respectively.  The KOSPI may have entered technical bear market but it remains one of the best-performing major global indexes this year, up 72%, while the S&P 500 is up 9.6% in 2026 so far.

content.title: SK Hynix, Samsung Drag KOSPI Into Bear Market as Memory-Chip Selloff Deepens
content.summary: The world’s hottest index has been dragged into a technical bear market as the memory-chip selloff gathered pace Wednesday.  The KOSPI index closed down 5.4% in South Korean trading as its two largest constituents  SK Hynix  and  Samsung Electronics  dropped 5.7% and 6.3%, respectively.  The KOSPI may have entered technical bear market but it remains one of the best-performing major global indexes this year, up 72%, while the S&P 500 is up 9.6% in 2026 so far.

headline: AI Chips Today - Certara-NVIDIA Partnership Revolutionizes AI in Drug Development
summary: Certara has announced a partnership with NVIDIA to enhance its AI platform for drug discovery and development by incorporating the NVIDIA BioNeMo Agent Toolkit. This collaboration aims to unify Certara's biosimulation software, regulatory expertise, and datasets with AI-driven agents that provide specialized insights for life sciences. The toolkit enables AI agents to function autonomously, complementing biosimulation models and scientific expertise to accelerate the drug development process...
